name: claude-code-usage-tracker-wb00
description: Track your Claude Code token usage and estimated cost from your local logs, right in the status bar.

commands.title: Claude Code Usage Tracker: Show Dashboard

claudeCodeUsageTracker.refreshIntervalSe.description: How often to refresh usage data, in seconds.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.currency.description: ISO currency code used to format cost estimates.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.decimalPlaces.description: Decimal places shown for cost figures.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showLimits.description: Show 5-hour and weekly plan-limit utilization in the status bar.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.useLiveApi.description: Fetch current plan limits live from Anthropic's usage endpoint (using the OAuth token in ~/.claude/.credentials.json) so they stay accurate even when Claude Code hasn't refreshed its on-disk cache. Falls back to the cache file if the live request fails. Turn off to read only the cache file.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.liveApiMinInterva.description: Minimum seconds between live usage-endpoint requests. The status bar still refreshes on its normal interval; this only throttles network calls to the limits API.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showOpusWeekly.description: Also show the weekly Opus limit (opus NN%) in the status bar, after the 5-hour and weekly figures. Requires Show Limits and a live weekly-Opus window.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showContext.description: Show the current session's context-window fill (like /context) in the status bar.

enumDescriptions: Group by the enclosing git repository.
claudeCodeUsageTracker.projectGroupingMo.description: How the dashboard's By project breakdown groups working directories.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showCost.description: Show today's estimated cost in the status bar.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showTokens.description: Show today's token count in the status bar.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.showExtraUsage.description: Show pay-as-you-go extra usage (spend beyond your plan limits) in the status bar and dashboard. Off by default; only appears when your account has extra usage enabled. Figures come from Anthropic's usage API.

claudeCodeUsageTracker.predictiveAlerts..description: Predictive alerts: estimate when you'll hit the 5-hour and weekly limits from your recent burn rate, show a time-to-limit ETA in the status bar, and warn before you run out. Turn off to disable all prediction features.
Trailing window, in minutes, used to measure your burn rate and how fast your plan-limit utilization is rising.
Utilization percentages at which to show a warning notification for either window. Each threshold fires once per window (re-armed when the window resets).
Also warn when your current pace projects you'll hit a limit before it resets (e.g. "~20m to the 5-hour limit, before it resets in 1h10m").

Show a 5-hour pace meter in the status bar: the projected end-of-window utilization at your current pace (e.g. `5h 24% → 48%`). A live 'how hard am I working' gauge that climbs toward 100% as you burn faster. Turn off to hide it. (Set both this and Show Weekly Eta off to hide all forecasts.)

Show a time-to-limit ETA for the weekly window — but only when your pace would actually hit the weekly limit before it resets (the weekly number moves over days, so a constant readout would be noisy). Turn off to hide the weekly ETA entirely.

Model-cost advisor (off by default): when recent turns show heavy spend on an expensive model for little output, show a one-time, dismissible hint suggesting a cheaper model for routine work. Requires Predictive Alerts to be enabled.
